title: Top parent holds before-vs-after.md/timeline.md outside its own spec.md optional-doc allowlist; before-vs-after.md is a forbidden-class change narrative

findingDetails.evidence: spec.md:87 names the permitted optional parent docs as exactly 'context-index.md migration bridge and resource-map.md'; spec.md:50-52 FORBIDS merge/migration/consolidation narratives at parent level. Yet `find -maxdepth 1` shows the parent root also contains before-vs-after.md (527 lines, a 'What Changed... Before/After/Impact' change narrative per its lines 1-60) and timeline.md (440 lines), plus handover.md — none named in the line 87 allowlist. validate.sh does not flag these, so it is undetected by tooling.
findingDetails.why: The parent control doc's stated content discipline contradicts its actual contents: it forbids before/after consolidation narratives at parent level and enumerates a narrower optional-doc set than what exists. A reader trusting spec.md:87 to know the parent's docs is misinformed, and before-vs-after.md occupies a content class the same file declares forbidden — a self-inconsistent lean-trio/content-discipline state. Low severity: validator-tolerated, files are deliberately cross-referenced (context-index.md:7, handover.md:80), no behavior impact.
findingDetails.recommendation: Either (a) relocate before-vs-after.md to a changelog rollup / child and keep only navigational bridges at the parent, or (b) if these are accepted reorg-bridge artifacts, update spec.md:87 to enumerate the full sanctioned parent-doc set (handover.md, context-index.md, resource-map.md, timeline.md, before-vs-after.md) and carve before-vs-after.md out of the FORBIDDEN content class at lines 50-52 so the discipline block stops contradicting the tree.
